arts.ttl: Predicted Teams Barcelona-Las Palmas: Hansi Flick to give young midfielder shot as Lamine Yamal returns
arts.sum: Barcelona are fresh off the back of their 125th anniversary celebrations on Friday night, and have an early start in the 14:00 CEST at Montjuic, with the reborn Las Palmas in town. The major team news for Barcelona is that Lamine Yamal and Ferran Torres are back in the squad. arts.ttl: Barça vs Las Palmas: Live Thread
arts.sum: The beautiful home of the world’s greatest football club in the capital of Catalonia is the site of a big La Liga match between visitors Las Palmas and Barcelona, who come into this one looking to return to winning ways in the league after getting just one point from their last two games. We don’t have a lot of rules here, but there are a few things to keep in mind when joining our matchday threads:

arts.ttl: Barcelona Announces Special 'Cat' Mascot to Celebrate 125th Anniversary
arts.sum: At the club's 125th anniversary celebrations yesterday, Barcelona unveiled their commemorative mascot, 'CAT', inspired by the Catalonia cat and created from the club's crest to reflect Barça’s most iconic features.

On Saturday 30 November at 2pm CET the Estadi Olímpic Lluís Companys will be the venue for week 15 in La Liga which sees Barça take on UD Las Palmas, a game in which the fans will be able to celebrate the 125th anniversary of FC Barcelona. Such occasions warrant big things, and the club pulled out all the stops at Friday's Anniversary Gala at the Gran Teatre del Liceu, the city's iconic opera house that dates all the way back to 1837 and wouldn't even let two fires get in the way of its survival.

arts.ttl: Pep Guardiola sports scar from Feyenoord capitulation as Manchester City boss sings during appearance at Barcelona's 125th anniversary gala
arts.sum: Barcelona stars of the past and present came together on Friday night to celebrate the 125th anniversary of the club but it was the appearance of Pep Guardiola that had everyone talking.

arts.sum: Brazilian footballing legend Ronaldinho scores a trademark free-kick for Barcelona against Real Madrid in a legends El Clasico match in Doha, Qatar.
